key_departures.impact: Rodgers has not committed to playing in 2026 and has not received a contract offer as of March 10. He piloted the Steelers to a 10-7 record and AFC North title in 2025 but the team enters free agency with only Mason Rudolph and 6th-round pick Will Howard under contract at QB. His departure/non-commitment is the defining open wound of this offseason.

Pro Bowl guard (2024) and three-year starting left guard signed 3yr/$31.5M with Arizona. Left Broderick Jones' blindside protection exposed — Jones himself is facing a long-term neck injury that may affect his 2026 availability. Departure is a significant loss to an already vulnerable left side of the OL.
2025 team MVP with 1,023 scrimmage yards signed 2yr/$14M with Tampa Bay. Steelers quickly replaced him with Rico Dowdle, so need impact is limited.
Started the final six games of the 2025 season after taking over for Darius Slay. Signed 2yr/$8.5M with Minnesota. Departure opened a hole that the Jamel Dean signing was designed to fill.

Cut on March 4, freeing $7M in cap space. His lone season in Pittsburgh was a disaster — averaged just 5.8 yards per reception and 4.1 yards per target. Release was a pure cap/roster cleanup. Freiermuth and Darnell Washington are back; this departure does not create a meaningful need.

Entered 2025 as WR2 but recorded just 372 yards in 14 games. His skillset doesn't fit McCarthy's system. No attempt made to re-sign. Departure is a minor depth loss, not a starter hole, given the Pittman trade.

signings.analysis: Trade acquired (late-round pick swap) plus immediate 3yr/$59M extension. Pittman had 80 catches for 784 yards and 7 TDs in 2025, his highest TD total yet. Pairs with DK Metcalf to give Pittsburgh one of the most reliable WR duos in the AFC — two big-bodied, route-running veterans. The Steelers lacked any WR2 threat in 2025 (Calvin Austin had 372 yards). This fully closes the WR2 hole. At $19.67M AAV, it's mid-tier-to-starter market. Guaranteed money not yet reported but contract structure signals real commitment. Note: slot/speed receiver depth still needed.
signings.draft_pick_note: Late-round pick swap with Indianapolis Colts. Minimal draft capital cost.

signings.analysis: 3yr/$36.75M is the second-largest FA contract in Steelers history. Dean had 3 INTs in 14 starts in 2025 — his best season as a pro — and quarterbacks posted a league-worst 46.9 passer rating targeting him (min. 443 coverage snaps). Slots in as boundary CB2 opposite Joey Porter Jr., creating a legitimate starting corner duo. Replaces James Pierre and addresses the CB2 need. Injury history (hip, knee, back issues, missed 3 games in 2025) is a concern but contract is appropriately priced. At $12.25M AAV it's middle of the starter tier for CB. Need is fully addressed for 2026. Note: guarantees not yet reported.

2yr/$12.25M replaces Gainwell. Dowdle had back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons (1,079 in 2024 for Cowboys, 1,076 in 2025 for Panthers) and reunites with head coach Mike McCarthy who coached him in Dallas. He joins Jaylen Warren (under contract, 958 rushing yards in 2025) and 2nd-year RB Kaleb Johnson to form a capable three-back rotation. RB room is fully stocked. At $6.125M AAV this is starter-tier money for RB. The need created by Gainwell's departure is fully addressed.

One-year, $4M re-signing. Samuel joined Pittsburgh mid-season in 2025 from the practice squad after spinal fusion surgery recovery, started 3 of 6 games and posted 10 tackles and 1 INT. Still a reclamation project at 26. The contract structure (1-year, depth money) signals he's CB4 on the depth chart behind Porter, Dean, and Echols — not a starter solution but valuable depth. With Dean already signed as the starter, this is a low-risk add with upside.

2yr/$5M re-signing. Holcomb is returning from a devastating multi-ligament knee injury sustained in 2023; played 14 games in 2025 mostly as a special teams contributor (161 ST snaps) with 37 tackles and 3 starts. At $2.5M AAV this is below the depth tier at LB. Pittsburgh already has Patrick Queen, Payton Wilson, and Malik Harrison under contract. Holcomb is LB4/5 depth and a ST ace. The deal makes sense if Harrison (potential cap casualty at $6.79M) is released. Minimal impact on overall LB need picture.

1-year, veteran-minimum re-signing. Rotational DL who appeared in 12 games in 2025 with 9 tackles and 1 sack, 5 pressures. Provides interior depth behind Cam Heyward, Keeanu Benton, and Derrick Harmon. Low cost continuity move — does not address any starter-level IDL need. IDL depth need unchanged.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Aaron Rodgers remains uncommitted and has not received a contract offer as of March 10. The Steelers have only Mason Rudolph and 2025 6th-round pick Will Howard under contract. QB remains the #1 need of this draft class and offseason. Draft a franchise QB in round 1 or 2 regardless of Rodgers' decision.

Isaac Seumalo's departure to Arizona (3yr/$31.5M) leaves the left guard spot vacated. LT Broderick Jones is facing a serious neck injury (fusion surgery) that clouds his 2026 availability. Spencer Anderson is the next man up at LG but is backup-caliber. The entire left side of the OL is now an urgent need — escalate IOL to tier 1.

Trade for Michael Pittman Jr. (3yr/$59M) fills the WR2 void opposite DK Metcalf. Pittsburgh now has a legitimate starter-quality WR1 and WR2. Depth/slot WR remains a need but it's no longer a premium priority. Drop from tier 1 to tier 3 (depth need). A slot/speed receiver in rounds 3-5 would still be welcome.

Jamel Dean (3yr/$36.75M) fills the CB2 role opposite Joey Porter Jr., replacing James Pierre. With Porter, Dean, Brandin Echols (slot), and Asante Samuel Jr. in the fold, CB is no longer a top need. Drop to tier 3. A developmental CB on Day 3 of the draft is fine but not urgent.
Rico Dowdle (2yr/$12.25M) replaces Gainwell alongside Jaylen Warren and Kaleb Johnson. Three-deep with two proven 1,000-yard runners. RB is not a draft need. Drop to tier 4.

Broderick Jones' neck fusion surgery creates serious uncertainty at LT — GM Omar Khan would not confirm Jones would be healthy for training camp. This was already a concern pre-free-agency and no OT was added. Combined with Seumalo's IOL departure, the entire left side of the offensive line is now critical. Escalate OT to tier 1.

Safety remains a need. Jalen Ramsey is expected to transition to free safety full-time now that Dean is the CB2. DeShon Elliott is returning from a torn ACL on an expiring deal. Multiple other safeties (Jabrill Peppers, Kyle Dugger, Chuck Clark) are hitting free agency. No safety signing made yet. Need unchanged — remains tier 2.

Jonnu Smith's release was addition-by-subtraction. Freiermuth and Darnell Washington form a legitimate starting duo. Connor Heyward is a pending free agent at TE/FB; his status is unclear. Depth TE need remains but unchanged at tier 3.

key_additions.headline: Reliable WR2 (80 rec, 784 yds, 7 TD in 2025) acquired via trade for minimal draft capital, instantly pairs with DK Metcalf for Pittsburgh's best WR duo in years
Elite boundary corner (3 INTs, league-worst 46.9 QB rating against) locks in as CB2 opposite Joey Porter Jr., addressing Pittsburgh's most urgent defensive need
Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher reunites with HC Mike McCarthy, replacing Gainwell in a deep Pittsburgh backfield alongside Jaylen Warren
Former Chargers standout re-signed as valuable CB depth after flashing in six mid-season games while continuing his recovery from spinal fusion surgery
Veteran LB re-signed as proven special teams contributor and depth piece in his second year removed from a devastating multi-ligament knee injury
Rotational interior DL retained on a minimum deal to provide depth behind Heyward, Benton, and Harmon

key_losses.headline: Pro Bowl LG departs for Arizona, leaving a critical hole on the left side of the OL with LT Broderick Jones also dealing with neck fusion surgery uncertainty
42-year-old QB who led Steelers to 10-7 record and AFC North title in 2025 remains uncommitted as of March 10, leaving Pittsburgh's most important roster question unresolved
2025 team MVP (1,023 scrimmage yards) cashes in with Tampa Bay; quickly replaced in Pittsburgh by Rico Dowdle
Starter over the final six games of 2025 departs for Minnesota; Jamel Dean signing directly replaces his role as CB2
Disastrous one-year tenure ends with a pre-free-agency cut; Smith averaged just 4.1 yards per target in Pittsburgh after arriving via the Jalen Ramsey trade

notes: All signings reflect the legal tampering period (March 9-10, 2026). Contracts cannot be officially signed until March 11 at 4 PM ET (new league year). Guaranteed money on Dean, Pittman, Dowdle, and Holcomb deals has not yet been officially reported — noted as 'TBD' where applicable. Esezi Otomewo contract value sourced from Spotrac ($1.03M). Pre-FA cap space sourced from OverTheCap ($46.2M). Post-FA cap space is estimated based on known AAV commitments and will be updated once official cap hits are processed. Aaron Rodgers' status is the most consequential unresolved item — his decision will significantly affect how the remaining cap space (~$20M+ estimated) is deployed and how the team approaches the April draft.
